# Report Template

Exact structure the skill emits to `workspace-agents-refine-<YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS>.md` at the workspace root. The skill fills every placeholder in `{{ double braces }}`; it never omits a section (empty sections print "No findings.").

The timestamp guarantees same-day reruns do not collide; never overwrite an existing report.

---

```markdown
# {{workspace-name}} — Agent Refinement Proposal

**Date:** {{YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM}}
**Generated by:** `workspace-agents-refine` skill
**Apply mode:** Propose-only. No edits were made to agents, `workspace.py`, `settings.json`, or rules. The user applies the diffs manually.

---

## 1. Executive summary

- **Agents audited:** {{N}}
- **Findings by severity:** {{N_critical}} critical, {{N_warn}} warn, {{N_info}} info
- **New agents proposed:** {{N_new_agents}} ({{"telemetry-grounded" | "heuristic-only"}})
- **Estimated token savings:** {{saved_tokens_total}} tokens across the telemetry window (if proposals applied)
- **Files the user must edit manually to apply all proposals:** {{file_list}}

One-paragraph summary (<=120 words) of the most consequential findings and the recommended apply order.

---

## 2. Per-agent findings

### 2.{{i}} `{{agent-name}}`

**File:** `.claude/agents/{{agent-name}}.md` ({{line_count}} lines, ~{{token_estimate}} tokens)

| 4 | Orchestrator top-level constraint | {{pass/fail/NA}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 5 | Scope Boundary | {{pass/fail/NA}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 6 | Knowledge-First Protocol | {{pass/fail/NA}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 7 | Pattern Recording | {{pass/fail/NA}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 8 | Write-tool discipline | {{pass/fail}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 9 | Body length proportional | {{pass/fail}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 10 | Act-and-scope directive | {{pass/fail}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |
| 11 | Evidence directive | {{pass/fail}} | {{severity}} | {{one-line evidence}} |

**Tool audit:**

| Tool | Source (`.md` / `workspace.py`) | Baseline expectation | Classification | Proposed action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| {{tool}} | {{present-in}} | {{baseline}} | {{adopt/preserve-with-reason/remove}} | {{action or "none"}} |

**Proposed `.md` diff** (copy-paste into `Edit`):

```diff
--- a/.claude/agents/{{agent-name}}.md
+++ b/.claude/agents/{{agent-name}}.md
@@ ... @@
{{unified diff}}
```

**Proposed `workspace.py` diff** (only present when workspace is Artisyn-managed and tool list or model needs to change):

```diff
--- a/workspace.py
+++ b/workspace.py
@@ ... @@
{{unified diff}}
```

**Notes:** {{free-form rationale pointing at specific rubric items and references}}

---
(repeat section 2.i for each of the N agents; include every agent even if clean — print "No findings." when clean)

{{unified diff or "No changes."}}

| Hook | Current state | Proposed action |

If bash-guard is missing, inline the full script from `references/bash-guard.md` here — do not reference it as a separate file the user needs to fetch.

---

## 4. New-agent proposals

### 4.{{j}} `{{proposed-agent-name}}` (split from `{{parent}}`)

**Gate calculation:**

```
parent_body_tokens     = {{N}}
invocations_total      = {{N}}  ({{"from telemetry" | "fallback"}})
sub_domain_share       = {{0.xx}}  ({{"from grep over logs" | "fallback 0.3"}})
new_agent_body_tokens  = {{N}}  (estimate)
parent_delegation_tokens = {{N}}  (estimate, per call)

baseline_cost          = {{N}}
post_split_cost        = {{N}}
saved_tokens_total     = {{N}}
gate_ratio             = saved_tokens_total / post_split_cost = {{x.xx}}
```

**Verdict:** {{"PROPOSE — gate_ratio >= 2.0" | "REJECT — gate_ratio < 2.0"}}

If proposed, the following artifacts are needed:

**Proposed `.claude/agents/{{proposed-agent-name}}.md`:**

```markdown
---
name: {{proposed-agent-name}}
description: "{{role sentence. Scope clause. NOT for X, use Y.}}"
tools: {{tools list, Claude-native}}
model: {{sonnet|opus|haiku}}
---

# {{Title}}

{{role paragraph}}

## Scope Boundary
...

## Knowledge-First Protocol
...

## Pattern Recording
...
```

**Proposed `workspace.py` addition** (only when workspace is Artisyn-managed):

```python
Agent(
    name="{{proposed-agent-name}}",
    description="{{same as .md description}}",
    prompt=W.prompt_ref("{{proposed-agent-name}}.md"),
    tools=[{{Artisyn-vocabulary list}}],
    allowedTools=[{{Artisyn-vocabulary list}}],
    resources=[
        "file://{{relevant-kb-path}}",
    ],
    model="{{sonnet|opus|haiku}}",
),
```

**Proposed `.claude/rules/routing.md` addition:**

```markdown
### {{routing topic}} -> {{proposed-agent-name}}
{{trigger phrases the orchestrator should route on}}
```

**Impact on parent (`{{parent}}`):**

- Body sections to remove from parent: {{list}}
- Lines removed from parent: ~{{N}}
- Parent's `description` clause to add: `"NOT for {{scope}} — use {{proposed-agent-name}}."`

---
(repeat 4.j for each proposal; if none clears the gate, print "No new-agent proposals clear the >=2x gate. Evaluated candidates:" followed by the rejection table with gate_ratio values.)

---

## 5. Baseline divergences preserved

Table of workspace-specific tool/permission extras that were intentionally kept despite diverging from the minimal baseline. The skill populates this from the evidence it found in Phase 3 (quoted text from agent bodies or `.claude/rules/*.md` that justifies the extra).

| Location | Item | Baseline says | Kept because | Evidence source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| {{agent}}.tools | {{extra tool}} | not in {{class}} baseline | {{one-sentence reason}} | {{file path and quoted line}} |

If a divergence is not in this table, it is a finding — see section 2 or 3.

---

## 6. Apply checklist

Apply in this order. Do **not** skip steps. When the workspace is Artisyn-managed, do **not** run `python3 workspace.py generate` (destructive to hand-tuned `.md` enrichments).

1. Review and apply proposed **body** diffs from section 2 (the anatomy items 10–14 and other body sections). Apply them to the **durable source**, not the generated file:
   - **Artisyn-managed workspace:** edit the prompt body source the agent's `W.prompt_ref("<name>.md")` points at — `prompts/<name>.md` — *not* `.claude/agents/<name>.md` (that file is regenerated). For a single agent you can apply the anatomy via the `author-agent` skill (confirm-before-apply). A **packaged system agent** has no in-workspace prompt source; its body ships in the platform template and is changed upstream, not here.
   - **Non-Artisyn workspace** (no `workspace.py`): `.claude/agents/<name>.md` *is* the source of truth — `Edit` the body diffs directly into it.
2. When the workspace is Artisyn-managed: review and apply frontmatter/tool/model `workspace.py` diffs from section 2 and the new-agent additions from section 4.
3. Apply `.claude/rules/routing.md` diffs from section 3.1 and 4.
4. Apply `.claude/settings.json` changes from section 3.3, including the bash-guard script drop (create `.claude/hooks/bash-guard.py` from the inline body; `chmod +x`).
5. When the workspace is Artisyn-managed, validate metadata for both providers:
   ```bash
   python3 workspace.py validate --agent claude
   python3 workspace.py validate --agent kiro
   ```
   Both must pass before committing. `delegation.md` is regenerated by `validate` where configured; do not hand-edit.
6. Smoke-test bash-guard:
   ```bash
   echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"rm -rf /"}}' | python3 .claude/hooks/bash-guard.py; echo exit=$?
   # Expected exit=2 with "bash-guard: blocked ..." on stderr
   ```
7. Re-invoke the skill. The new report must show zero `critical` and zero `warn` findings, or each remaining finding must carry a `<!-- baseline-divergence: <reason> -->` marker in the agent body.
8. Commit the changes as a single patch with message `chore(agents): apply refinement proposal {{YYYY-MM-DD}}`.
```

---

## Conventions the template enforces

- **Every unified diff is copy-paste-ready for `Edit`** — includes file header `--- a/<path>` / `+++ b/<path>`, includes enough context lines that `old_string` is unique.
- **Per-agent sections are always present** — even clean agents print a stub with "No findings." — so a diff of two reports is meaningful.
- **Gate numbers are always shown** — the >=2x check never appears as an assertion without numerator and denominator.
- **Baseline-divergence table is populated from evidence found in the workspace** — the skill does not invent reasons; it quotes them from agent bodies or rules files.
- **Report files never overwrite each other** — same-day re-runs suffix `-2`, `-3`, etc.
- **No external links** — the report is a local, diff-on-file artifact. It does not link to Jira, Confluence, or any URL.
